mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during elevated FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly more negative than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income growth and corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent, or Rank IC exceeds 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad contraction in both consumer demand and goods output, which depresses aggregate corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly more negative during joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO than during periods when only one series declines (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a federal funds rate above 4.5 percent actively restricts credit availability and raises borrowing costs across the economy, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent during tightening cycles, or Rank IC exceeds 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1400 thousand units signals weakness in residential construction and related consumer durables demand, which translates into lower broad economic momentum and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when HOUST falls below 1400, or Rank IC exceeds 0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rises in the unemployment rate identify a manufacturing-led recession where collapsing goods-sector output coincides with labor market deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns during joint INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise periods do not underperform single-factor periods by at least 3 percent annualized, or interaction Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls below 137500 thousand persons indicates a sudden contraction in aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances when PAYEMS falls below 137500, or Rank IC exceeds 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales levels reflect robust consumer expenditure that directly lifts corporate top-line revenues and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive over any 2-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03.
